summary: Executed full backend pytest suite plus focused auth/password-recovery/profile coverage, and ran Playwright UI/integration checks for login page Google auth UI, password recovery interactions, settings profile updates, and Google callback route behavior.

integration_issues.flow: Google auth callback with invalid or expired session_id
integration_issues.issue: App remains stuck on 'Opening Kindred / Completing your Google sign-in.' screen instead of recovering back to login when session exchange fails.

action_items: Fix Google callback failure handling in /app/frontend/src/App.js so failed /auth/google/session exchanges clear hash/processing state and redirect to /login.
Retest callback behavior with invalid session_id hash and with real provider callback after fix.
(Optional hardening) Add timeout/fallback UI action on callback screen to avoid infinite wait if third-party session exchange fails.
critical_code_review_comments: Blocking integration bug in App.js: when a hash session_id exists and /auth/google/session fails, catch path does not remove URL hash; hasGoogleSessionId remains true, causing permanent loading screen.
backend/server.py remains oversized (>700 lines) and should be modularized into domain routers/services to reduce regression risk.
seed_data_creation: Created TEST/UI auth communities via /api/auth/bootstrap and Launch UI flow using unique emails. Updated one UI test profile record through settings form.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend auth/profile/recovery coverage is now in /app/backend/tests/test_auth_google_recovery_profile.py and passes. Playwright validated login page Google button + recovery UI + launch/login/settings profile save. Repro remaining blocker: open /dashboard#session_id=fake-session-for-ui-test -> app shows 'Opening Kindred' indefinitely and never returns to login.
rca of the issue: RCA: In /app/frontend/src/App.js, when session_id exists, validateSession() calls /auth/google/session and only clears URL hash on success (window.history.replaceState at success path). On failure, catch clears local session but does not clear hash. Since hasGoogleSessionId is derived from window.location.hash including session_id, UI keeps rendering FullScreenMessage indefinitely. Reproduction: navigate to /dashboard#session_id=fake-session-for-ui-test; observe persistent loading card. Mitigation: in catch/finally, also clear hash (e.g., replaceState to pathname) and navigate unauthenticated users to /login.
